description: User sets a hard budget constraint for gifts ($50 max). Tests whether the system respects the dollar ceiling when making gift recommendations.

messages.content: I have a rule for myself: I never spend more than $50 on a gift. That's my hard limit, no exceptions.
Noted -- $50 is your maximum gift budget, no exceptions.
My coworker's birthday is coming up. She's into gardening. Any gift ideas?
How about a set of quality gardening gloves ($15-25) or a succulent arrangement ($20-35)?
The succulent arrangement is a great idea, thanks.
My sister's wedding anniversary is next month. I want to get her and her husband something nice.
Within your $50 budget, you could get a nice photo frame, a custom cutting board, or a gift card to their favorite restaurant.
The custom cutting board is a lovely idea.
My friend recommended this fancy candle set that costs $85. Tempting but I'll stick to my rule.
Good call sticking to your budget. There are great candle sets under $50 if you want alternatives.
Sure, send me some options under $50.
I heard my neighbor loves cooking. He's turning 40 next week.
For a cooking enthusiast, you could consider a spice set ($25-40), a quality wooden spoon set ($15-30), or a cookbook by a popular chef ($20-35).
All great. I'll go with the spice set.
My best friend just got engaged! I really want to get them something special. What do you suggest?

previous_values.value: Reinforced: declined $85 item, sticking to rule

probe.prompt: My best friend just got engaged! I really want to get them something special. What do you suggest?

interference.description: The probe involves a best friend's engagement -- a high-emotion occasion. System must not override the hard budget constraint due to the sentimental context.
Past sessions show gifts in the $15-40 range. The engagement context might tempt the system to suggest higher-end items that breach $50.
